text:
In SSAB, the quality of the base liquor used for production of the soy sauce-aroma type baijiu is the most important factor for the quality of the finished product. The identification and grading of the base liquors is also an important aspect of fermentation control in the baijiu production process. This task is currently based on the skill and expertise of few highly trained tasters. The work is often very demanding and somewhat subjective. The knowledge of the detailed chemical composition of the different liquor grades is also important. In lower grades of liquors one can be concerned about possible toxicological effects.

The objective of this work was to investigate the connection between the quality grades of the SSAB base liquors from the third, fourth and fifth fermentation rounds and the key aromatic compounds. A hybrid approach combining sensory evaluation, an electronic nose (E-nose) and headspace solid-phase microextraction gas chromatography-mass spectrometry (HS-SPME-GC-MS) was used to establish a comprehensive composition profile of key aromatic compounds of base liquors.

A large number of aroma compounds such as sauce, floral, fruity, and fermented aroma and the parameters indicating taste such as heavy taste, balance and persistence were more significant in the high grade quality assessment, which was also validated by E-nose for the first time to achieve the digitalized recognition of aroma between different grades. The untargeted metabolomic analysis by using HS-SPME-GC-MS in different fermentation levels showed that there were 16 common differential metabolites detected, including 11 kinds of esters, 1 alcohol, 2 aldehydes and 2 ketones, indicating that the aromatic compounds and fruity flavor of the high-grade base liquor were relatively larger, and a strong fruity flavor contributed more to the high-grade quality of the base liquor.

Further Correlation analysis confirmed that furfuryl ethyl ether, ethyl butanoate, isopentyl hexanoate, ethyl nonanoate and 3-methyl-1-butanol contributed the most to the E-nose sensor response. By means of identification of chemical key markers associated to the quantification of the digitized differences between the traditional sensory characteristics and their attributes in the steel product, this study enables to improve the mixing and storage conditions of SSAB products and hence to preserve quality and food safety.
